A cell where exactly one side is the float 0.0 and the other is NULL is this documented dense-vs-sparse encoding fork, classed METHOD-CHOICE across ALL variables and BOTH sidednesses via the ZERO_VS_NULL predicate: (published==0.0 AND built is NULL) OR (built==0.0 AND published is NULL). NOT counted as a match (METHOD-CHOICE not in MATCH_CLASSES) and NOT dropped from the derivable denominator -> cannot raise match_share; only moves the fork out of UNEXPLAINED into a named bounded class. Two-sided value divergences never satisfy the predicate and stay UNEXPLAINED.
